yogurt-and-zucchini-pastry-lezzet.name: Yogurt And Zucchini Pastry
instructions: 1. Peel and reinde the pumpkins for the inner mortar of the body.
2. Take a large bowl of grater.
3. Add and mix eggs, yogurt, sunflower oil and salt.
4. Take a rough pit.
5. Add milk, sunflower oil, baking powder and salt.
6. Azar azar knead until they get a smooth dough by adding warm water.
7. The dough is divided into two equal parts.
8. Take a piece of five cloths.
9. Open Cloths in 20-25 cm.
10. Place sunflower oil with brush on top.
11. 11. Open the Yufka group to be larger than two inches from the tray.
12. Remove sunflower oil on the tray and place the edges of the dough out of the tray.
12. August pouring and broadcasting on the inner tribute.
14 July Take the other half of the dough to eight glands.
15. Open Cloths in 20-25 cm.
16. Place sunflower oil with brush on top.
17. September Open the Yufka group to be five santims of the tray.
18. Cool on the inner mortar that emits the tray by diming the yufka you open.
19. September Place the bottom softener wrapped out of the tray on the top of the altar.
20. Spray sunflower oil on the flyer.
21. Preheated 180 degree bake and serve until six and above fried in the oven.
22.  Afiyet olsun.

yogurt-mousse-with-lemon-teleculinaria.name: Yogurt Mousse With Lemon
instructions: Drain the gelatin leaves in cold water. In a separate bowl add the yogurts with condensed milk and lemon juice and stir well. Drain the gelatin leaves, cause them to melt in the microwave without boiling, add to the bowl with the previous preparation and stir. Put it in the fridge and book it.
Beat the cream in whipped cream very firmly and wrap with the cream you prepared. Serve tastefully decorated with lemon zest.

yogurt-pie-with-grape-and-black-pepper-c.name: Yogurt Pie With Grape And Black-pepper Compote
instructions: Place a rack in middle of oven and preheat to 325°. Pulse gingersnaps in a food processor until very fine crumbs form (you should have about 2 cups). Add flour, raw sugar, and salt and pulse to combine. Add butter and pulse until mixture is the consistency of wet sand.

Transfer mixture to a 9” pie dish. Using a measuring cup, press firmly onto bottom and up sides of pie dish. Place pie dish on a rimmed baking sheet and bake crust, rotating halfway through, until dry and set, 20–25 minutes. Transfer pie dish to a wire rack and let crust cool.
DO AHEAD: Crust can be baked 1 day ahead. Store wrapped tightly at room temperature.
Place gelatin and 2 Tbsp. milk in a small bowl and let stand until gelatin is softened, 5–10 minutes.

Warm remaining ½ cup milk in a small saucepan over medium heat until steaming, about 3 minutes. Remove from heat, add gelatin mixture, and whisk until smooth. Whisk in yogurt, brown sugar, and lemon zest and pour into pie crust, smoothing top if needed; chill until set, at least 2 hours.

Meanwhile, bring grapes, granulated sugar, lemon juice, pepper, and salt to a boil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, stirring to dissolve sugar. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally, until grapes burst and mixture is syrupy, 8–10 minutes. Let cool.
Using a slotted spoon, spoon compote over pie slices just before serving.
DO AHEAD: Pie and compote can be made 2 days ahead; cover separately and chill.
